---
title: \textbf{Examining The Effect of Padding on Facial Recognition Using Convolution Neural Networks}
output:
  pdf_document:
    number_sections: TRUE
    keep_tex: TRUE
    fig_caption: TRUE
    latex_engine: xelatex
geometry: "left = 2.54cm, right = 2.54cm, top = 2.54cm, bottom = 2.54cm"
fontsize: 12pt
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{float}
  - \usepackage{sectsty}
  - \usepackage{paralist}
  - \usepackage{setspace}
  - \setstretch{2.0}
  - \usepackage{fancyhdr}
  - \usepackage{ragged2e}
  - \usepackage{lastpage}
  - \usepackage{dcolumn}
  - \usepackage[nottoc, numbib]{tocbibind}
  - \usepackage{enumitem}
  - \renewcommand{\theequation}{\thechapter.\arabic{equation}}
documentclass: report
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE} 
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```




\allsectionsfont{\centering}
\subsectionfont{\raggedright}
\subsubsectionfont{\raggedright}

\pagenumbering{gobble}

\maketitle

\begin{titlepage}
\centering
\Large \textbf{\ Examining The Effect of Padding on Facial Recognition Using Convolution Neural Networks}
\vspace*{\fill}
```{r uni_logo, echo=F, out.width="80%"}
knitr::include_graphics("img/jkuatlogo.png")
```
\vspace{1cm}

\normalsize
Wycliffe Atswenje Sikolia \vspace{0.3cm}

A Research Proposal Submitted in Partial Fulfillment of The Requirements for The Award of a Post Graduate Diploma in Applied Statistics of Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology.\vspace{1cm}

2024 

\vspace*{\fill}
\end{titlepage}

\newpage

\pagenumbering{roman}

\begin{centering}

{\bf DECLARATION}

\end{centering}

\normalsize

This research proposal is my own work and has not been presented elsewhere for a degree award.

Signature ........................................ Date   ..........................  

Wycliffe Atswenje Sikolia  

SCB384-3284/2013 

**Declaration by supervisors**  

This research proposal has been submitted for examination with our approval as university supervisors.

Signature ........................................ Date   .......................... 

Dr. Anthony Kibira Wanjoya  

Department of Statistics and Actuarial Sciences  

Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology 


Signature ........................................        Date   ..........................

Dr. Jane Aduda  

Department of Statistics and Actuarial Sciences  

Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology

\newpage

\begin{centering}

{\bf DEDICATION}

\end{centering}
\normalsize
To my Family and Friends.

\newpage

\begin{centering}

{\bf ACKNOWLEDGEMENT}

\end{centering}
\normalsize
Special regards to my supervisors Dr. Anthony Kibira Wanjoya and Dr. Jane Aduda for their guidance and relentless criticism on this work. Their continuous support, insightful comments, and demand for excellence have played a crucial role in this work. I am particularly grateful to my father whose moral support has been instrumental in my studies. My friends and family have also helped during the course of my studies by reminding me of the need to focus and complete my studies, which is the reason I got this far. Their words of positive affirmation gave me a shoulder to lean on when the going got difficult. I would also like to thank members of the department of statistics and actuarial sciences from the Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology for their support in my studies. Most importantly I would like to thank the almighty God for granting me the knowledge to carry out this study


\newpage

\begin{centering}

{\bf ABSTRACT}

\end{centering}

\onehalfspacing
\normalsize
Facial recognition technology is the development of solutions that can match a face from an image or a real time video to that of a known person. While such technology exists, no research has been done to examine the effect of padding on facial recognition. This study aims to contribute to the existing literature by developing a facial recognition solution by comparing the effect of padding using a self-built model and a pre-trained model. It follows that the study will add value to the existing knowledge by creating a guideline that could be used when there is need to make a decision between creating an accurate model and saving on the training time.


\newpage
\centering
\raggedright
\newpage
\normalsize
\onehalfspacing
\tableofcontents


\onehalfspacing
\normalsize
\newpage
\listoffigures





\newpage
\onehalfspacing
\normalsize
\listoftables




\newpage

\large

\doublespacing

\begin{centering}

{\bf List of Abbreviations and Acronyms}

\end{centering}



\newpage

\pagenumbering{arabic}

```{r intro, child = 'introduction.Rmd'}
```

\newpage

```{r litret, child = 'literature.Rmd'}
```


\newpage


```{r methods, child = 'methodology.Rmd'}
```
